mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or the signal's Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to price in lower corporate profits and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power with a Rank IC below 0.03 during periods of stable inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing and utility output, which triggers short-term momentum buying and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day returns in >60% of instances during supply-constrained periods, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of restrictive federal funds rates above 4.0% and an unemployment rate exceeding 4.0% creates a hard-landing risk premium, forcing equity deleveraging and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >50% of triggered periods, or the joint condition fails to separate winning and losing months with a t-statistic below 1.5 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product confirms macroeconomic expansion, which validates corporate earnings forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth coincides with negative 20-day equity returns in >65% of observations, or the signal's Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.3% triggers immediate expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in >55% of high-inflation months, or the signal loses predictive power when core inflation remains below 2.5% over consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a strengthening labor market, which boosts consumer spending expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive returns when wage growth decouples from employment, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in walk-forward tests.
